title: NIS2 Risk Analysis Evidence for Important Entities

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2) is assigned contributing assurance because this UC provides concrete Splunk evidence for the observable control condition, while policy approval, legal interpretation, and official filings remain outside Splunk.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.22: NIS2 Risk Analysis Evidence for Important Entities.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.22' running on nis2_entity_classification.csv (entity_tier=important), qualys:hostdetection, authentication CIM, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=qualys` `sourcetype=qualys:hostdetection` exposes `qid`, `severity`, `first_found_datetime`, `host`. Important-tier assets are filtered via `nis2_entity_classification.csv` columns `asset_hostname`, `business_service`, `entity_tier`. Supplemental context can be pulled from `index=grc` `sourcetype=grc:risk` (`risk_id`, `likelihood`) and `index=auth` with `sourcetype=OktaIM2:log` for access spikes. Splunk Add-on for Qualys (Splunkbase 2964); Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) optional for dashboards.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (Splunkbase 2964), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv WHERE entity_tier="important"
| rename asset_hostname as host
| join host type=left [
    search index=qualys sourcetype="qualys:hostdetection" earliest=-30d
    | eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(first_found_datetime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))/86400,0)
    | where severity IN ("Critical","High") AND age_days > 30
    | stats dc(qid) as aged_findings by host
]
| where isnotnull(aged_findings) AND aged_findings > 0
| table host, business_service, aged_findings
| sort - aged_findings
description: Flags important-tier hosts whose critical or high Qualys findings are older than the proportionate SLA, proving lighter-weight but time-bound risk analysis stays honest without essential-tier overhead.
value: Supports fair regulatory storytelling for important entities by translating proportionality into concrete telemetry—aged backlog counts by `business_service`—so teams can explain why controls are right-sized, not merely absent.
implementation: (1) Maintain `nis2_entity_classification.csv` with accurate hostnames for important entities. (2) Keep Qualys connector (Splunkbase 2964) healthy; alert on ingest breaks separately. (3) Tune `age_days > 30` to national risk policy. (4) Export monthly management review decks from Splunk. (5) Sync exceptions through `nis2_qualys_exception_register.csv` with expiry.
visualization: Bar chart of aged findings by host, table of worst offenders, single value counting hosts past SLA.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest, Vulnerabilities.severity | sort - count
knownFalsePositives:
• **Qualys scanning paused on satellite plants** — Distinguish: detectors show aged `first_found_datetime` because no fresh scan arrived. Suppress: join `nis2_scan_blackout.csv` with `scan_paused=true` for the host during the outage window.
• **Accepted qualys QIDs** — Distinguish: backlog is intentional with compensating control tickets. Suppress: lookup `nis2_qid_exception_register.csv` listing `(host,qid)` pairs with `approved_until`.
• **Hostname case mismatch** — Distinguish: Qualys emits `HOST.EXAMPLE` while CSV uses lower case. Suppress: normalize with `| eval host=lower(host)` before the join and in the lookup staging query.
• **Transient cloud ephemerals** — Distinguish: autoscale nodes disappear mid-cycle. Suppress: filter `| lookup nis2_cloud_transient.csv host OUTPUT transient | where transient!="yes"`.
• **Reclassification from important to essential** — Distinguish: stale CSV still lists `entity_tier=important`. Suppress: weekly automated reload from ServiceNow CMDB via Splunkbase 1928 export job.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Qualys subscription with API user allowed for Splunk Add-on (Splunkbase 2964); forwarder or HF with outbound HTTPS **443** to Qualys platform.
• CSV `nis2_entity_classification.csv` with `entity_tier` values normalized (`important` lowercase).
• Index `qualys` on indexers with **90-day** retention minimum for trend proof.
• Splunk ES (Splunkbase 263) optional asset merges if host names must align to `cmdb_ci`.
• RBAC: vulnerability analysts receive `read` on `index=qualys` only.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Install the Qualys TA, enable modular inputs per vendor guide, set `polling_interval=300` seconds for knowledge-base heavy tenants. Confirm raw events show `first_found_datetime` ISO-8601. If timestamps differ, add `TIME_FORMAT` in `props.conf`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste SPL, run across `earliest=-30d`, verify counts vs Qualys UI. Save as `UC-22.2.22`. Alert daily digest only if `aged_findings>10` to limit noise.

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv WHERE entity_tier="important"
| rename asset_hostname as host
| join host type=left [
    search index=qualys sourcetype="qualys:hostdetection" earliest=-30d
    | eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(first_found_datetime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))/86400,0)
    | where severity IN ("Critical","High") AND age_days > 30
    | stats dc(qid) as aged_findings by host
]
```

Understanding this SPL: SLA days and severity filters implement proportionality; widen window only after CISO approval.

Step 3 — Validate
Choose three hosts from the result. In Qualys UI, confirm QID ages exceed policy. Capture screenshots for audit workbook. Optionally compare to `index=auth` login spikes indicating compensating monitoring.

Step 4 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot
Dashboard Studio layout: headline single value, detail table, map by `business_service`. If join misses data, run `| metadata type=hosts index=qualys` to catch spelling drift.

Step 5 — Governance
Quarterly review of exceptions register. Archive Splunk PDF exports with `evidenceSigning`. When entity moves to essential, retire this UC’s row via CSV change management.
grandmaExplanation: For the slightly-smaller-but-still-regulated group, we watch whether serious known software problems are left sitting too long, so we can say our follow-up matches the lighter rules that apply to them.
exclusions: Does not determine legal applicability, complete regulator submissions, approve policy decisions, or replace counsel/competent-authority review.
evidence: `UC-22.2.22` saved search output, Qualys correlation notes, and signed CSV extracts showing `host`, `aged_findings`, captured in `audit_evidence` for important-entity backlog review.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Mark `important-host-01` in the lookup, ingest Qualys events with `severity=High`, `first_found_datetime` 45 days old, rerun: expect the host in results with `aged_findings>=1`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Patch the issue so Qualys clears the detection or refresh `first_found_datetime` inside 30 days and rerun the same window: expect zero matching rows for that host when SLA is met.
